summary: DHH announces his preparations for OSCON 2005, where he will deliver a keynote, a session talk, and a tutorial. He notes the long travel from Denmark and plans to arrive early for a weekend of hacking in Portland. He highlights the strong Ruby presence at the conference, with luminaries like Matz, Dave Thomas, and _why attending alongside the Rails core team. He also expresses excitement about the upcoming Agile Web Development with Rails book potentially being ready by then.
key_points: DHH is presenting three times at OSCON 2005: a keynote, session talk, and tutorial
The Ruby community will have a major presence with key figures like Matz, Dave Thomas, and _why
The Agile Web Development with Rails book is expected to launch around the same time
Rails was gaining enough momentum by mid-2005 to warrant a keynote slot at a major open source conference
money_quotes: It's going to be a fantastic show for Ruby.
We'll have Matz, Dave Thomas, _why, and most of the Rails core group too.
With enough perseverance, the book will be hot off the press too.
key_insight: By mid-2005, Rails had achieved enough prominence in the open source world to earn DHH a keynote at OSCON, marking a milestone moment for both Rails and the broader Ruby community.
